_metadata.note: Historical tax change timelines for state profiles. Populated from public records and state revenue department histories.

MN.event: Individual income tax enacted (one of the earliest states)
Sales tax enacted at 3%, gradually raised to 6.875%
Fourth income tax bracket added (8.5% on high earners)
Top rate raised to 9.85% on income over ~$156,000 (adjusted annually)
Major tax bill — Child Tax Credit enacted, additional property tax refund, K-12 credit expanded

WI.event: Wisconsin becomes one of the first states to adopt an individual income tax
Sales tax enacted at 3%, raised to 5% in 1982
2011–2015: Significant tax cuts under Governor Walker, including income tax rate reductions
2023–2025: Legislative proposals to flatten income tax to single rate; none enacted as of early 2026

IA.event: Tax reform legislation signed, beginning phased rate reductions
Governor signs legislation converting to flat tax, phased 2023-2026
Flat rate of 3.9% takes effect, completing transition from 8.98% top rate

GA.event: Individual income tax enacted with graduated rates
Sales tax enacted at 3%, raised to 4% in 1989
Converted from graduated (top rate 5.75%) to flat 5.49%
Rate reduced to 5.39%, with scheduled reductions to 4.99% floor

CA.event: Individual income tax enacted with graduated rates
Proposition 13 passes — caps property tax rate at 1%, limits assessment increases to 2%/year
Mental Health Services Act (Prop 63) adds 1% surcharge on income over $1M
Proposition 30 temporarily raises top rate to 13.3% (highest in nation)
Proposition 55 extends Prop 30 top rates through 2030
Mental Health Services Tax extended. CalEITC expanded.

TX.event: Sales tax enacted at 2%, gradually raised to 6.25% by 1990
Franchise tax reformed to include partnerships and corporations
Constitutional amendment prohibits personal income tax without voter approval
Franchise tax replaced with Margins Tax (reformed business tax)
$18 billion property tax relief package signed. Franchise tax threshold raised.

FL.event: State constitution prohibits individual income tax
Sales tax rate raised to 4%, reaching current 6% by 1988
Save Our Homes amendment caps homestead assessment increases at 3%/year
Homestead exemption increased. Corporate rate held at 5.5%.

NY.event: Individual income tax enacted — one of the first states
'Millionaire's tax' surcharge enacted on high earners
Tax reform restructures brackets, top rate initially set to expire
Top rate raised to 10.9% on income over $25M, new brackets added
Top rate of 10.9% extended through 2027. PTET expanded.

IL.event: Individual income tax enacted at flat 2.5% (flat from inception)
Permanent rate of 4.95% enacted, ending budget impasse
Graduated tax ballot measure (Fair Tax Amendment) fails — voters reject switch from flat rate

TN.event: Hall Tax enacted — tax on interest and dividend income only
Sales tax enacted at 2%, gradually raised to 7% by 2002
Hall Tax phase-out begins, reducing rate from 6% by 1%/year
Hall Tax fully eliminated — Tennessee becomes true no-income-tax state

NC.event: Modern income tax enacted with graduated rates
Major reform: graduated income tax replaced with flat 5.8%, corporate rate cuts begin
SB 105: Phased rate reductions from 5.25% toward 3.99% by 2027; corporate phase-out by 2030
Flat rate reaches 4.25%, on track for 3.99% in 2026

WA.event: Business and Occupation (B&O) tax enacted — gross receipts tax, not income tax
Sales tax rate set at 4.5%, reaching current 6.5% by 1983
Income tax ballot initiative (I-1098) rejected by voters
Capital gains tax of 7% enacted on gains over $250,000
State Supreme Court upholds capital gains tax (classified as excise tax)
Capital gains tax rate increased to 9.9%

AL.event: Individual income tax enacted with graduated rates (2% to 5%)
Constitutional amendment increased allowable corporate income tax rate to 5%
Single sales factor apportionment adopted for business income
Grocery sales tax reduced; PTET election process simplified

AK.event: Individual income tax enacted at 10% of federal tax liability
Alaska Permanent Fund established, investing 25% of petroleum revenue
Individual income tax repealed after Trans-Alaska Pipeline oil revenue surge (only state to repeal income tax)
Oil tax reformed under SB 21, replacing ACES system

AZ.event: Individual income tax and Transaction Privilege Tax (sales tax) both enacted
Legislature passed flat income tax legislation
Flat 2.5% individual income tax rate takes full effect (down from graduated rates topping ~4.5%)
Corporate income tax rate reduced to 4.9%

AR.event: Individual income tax enacted (Hall Net Income Tax Law) at top rate of 5%
Income tax converted to flat 4.9% rate, beginning reduction trend
Top rate reduced to 3.9%; grocery sales tax eliminated effective Jan 1, 2026

CO.event: Income tax converted from graduated rates to flat 5.0% rate
TABOR enacted — constitutional amendment limiting revenue growth, requiring voter approval for tax increases
SB 24-228 creates TABOR-linked rate reduction mechanism; temporary rate of 4.25%

CT.event: Individual income tax enacted at flat 4.5% under Gov. Weicker; sales tax cut from 8% to 6%
Dual-bracket rate structure introduced (replacing flat rate)
Top rate raised to 6.99% on income over $500,000; bracket count expanded
PTET made elective (was mandatory). Bottom two marginal rates reduced. Estate tax exemption raised to near-federal levels.

DE.event: Modern personal income tax enacted (graduated rates, 2.2%-6.6%)
Selectively decoupled from OBBBA provisions. No PTET enacted (one of five income-tax states without one).

DC.event: Individual income tax first enacted (DC Income Tax Act of 1939)
Top rate raised to 10.75%; earned income credit increased
Sales tax increased from 6.0% to 6.5% (rising to 7.0% in Oct 2026)

HI.event: Income taxes among earliest in US territory
General Excise Tax (GET) enacted at 4% — functions like a sales tax but levied on businesses
Statehood; existing territorial tax system carried forward
Act 46 (Green Affordability Plan II): Widened all 12 income brackets; doubled standard deduction. Act 50 reduced PTET rate.

ID.event: Individual and corporate income taxes enacted
Converted from graduated income tax to flat 5.8% rate
Flat rate reduced to 5.3% (third consecutive annual cut)

IN.event: Individual income tax, corporate income tax, and retail sales tax all enacted simultaneously
Major restructuring: property tax caps constitutionally enacted; sales tax raised to 7%
Individual income tax rate reduced to 3.05% (continuing phased reductions from 3.23%)

KS.event: 'Kansas Experiment' — top rate cut from 6.45% to 4.9%; pass-through income exempted; massive revenue shortfall followed
Legislature overrides veto to repeal 2012 tax cuts; top rate raised to 5.7%
SB 1: Consolidated 3 brackets to 2; top rate reduced to 5.58%; Social Security fully exempt

KY.event: Individual income tax established with graduated rates (2%-6%)
Income tax converted to flat 5.0%; sales tax extended to many previously exempt services
HB 8 enacted — trigger mechanism for phased reductions toward elimination
Rate reduced to 4.0% (third trigger met). On track for 3.5% in 2026.

LA.event: Individual income tax enacted with graduated rates
Major overhaul: flat 3% income tax (replacing graduated 1.85%-4.25%); corporate flattened to 5.5%; sales tax raised to 5%; franchise tax repealed (2026)

ME.event: Individual and corporate income taxes enacted
3% income tax surcharge on income over $200,000 enacted then repealed same year
Child tax credit doubled for children under 6. PTET bill introduced but not enacted.

MD.event: Sales tax rate increased from 5% to 6%
Two new top income tax brackets added (6.25%, 6.50%). New 2% capital gains surcharge for AGI over $350K. New 3% IT services sales tax.

MA.event: Individual income tax enacted (among earliest states)
Proposition 2-1/2 enacted — caps property tax levies at 2.5% of assessed value
Voters approve reducing income tax rate to 5% (took until 2020 to fully implement)
'Millionaire\'s tax' (4% surtax on income over ~$1M) takes effect via 2022 ballot measure

MI.event: Individual income tax enacted at flat 2.6%
Proposal A: sales tax raised to 6% to fund schools; income tax reduced; property tax reformed
Income tax temporarily raised to 4.35% during fiscal crisis; gradually reduced back to 4.25%
Decoupled from OBBBA provisions. FTE tax election deadline extended. Tipped/overtime deductions enacted.

MS.event: Sales tax first enacted (Mississippi was the first state to adopt a general sales tax)
HB 531 converts graduated income tax to flat 5% rate
'Build Up Mississippi Act' (HB 1) — phases income tax to 3% by 2030 with trigger toward elimination; grocery tax cut

MO.event: Hancock Amendment — caps state revenue as percentage of personal income, requires voter approval for tax increases
Phased income tax rate cuts enacted; top rate gradually reduced from 6% toward 4.7%
Top rate reduced to 4.7% via revenue trigger. 100% capital gains exclusion enacted.

MT.event: Structural tax reform — rates reduced, 16 tax credits repealed, corporate apportionment reformed
Income tax simplified to two-bracket system (4.7% and 5.9%)

NE.event: Individual income tax, corporate income tax, and sales tax all enacted simultaneously
LB 754 — largest income tax cut in state history; top rate phased from 6.64% to 3.99% by 2027
Debate over pausing scheduled rate cuts due to revenue shortfalls

NV.event: Sales and Use Tax Act enacted at 2%
Commerce Tax enacted — gross receipts tax on businesses with Nevada revenue over $4M
No income tax (constitutionally prohibited). Revenue depends on gaming taxes, sales tax, and payroll-based Modified Business Tax.

NH.event: Interest and Dividends (I&D) Tax enacted at 5% (only form of individual income tax)
Legislature passes phase-out of I&D Tax
I&D Tax fully repealed — NH becomes true no-income-tax state

NJ.event: Individual income tax enacted following court order on school funding
Top rate of 8.97% established for income over $500K
Top rate raised to 10.75% on income over $5M
Corporate transit fee proposed. Child and dependent care credit expanded.

NM.event: Gross Receipts Tax enacted, replacing Emergency School Tax
GRT rate reduced from 5.125% to 5%, further to 4.875% in 2023
Income tax restructured to 6 brackets (1.5%-5.9%) under HB 252

ND.event: Individual income tax enacted (first among neighboring states)
HB 1158 — largest income tax cut in state history; brackets compressed from 5 to 3; first $55,975 tax-free

OH.event: HB 66: Corporate franchise/tangible property tax phased out; Commercial Activity Tax (CAT) phased in; 21% income tax cut
CAT exclusion threshold raised; top income tax rate cut to 3.5%
Rate reduced to 3.5%. CAT threshold raised to $6M, eliminating it for most small businesses.

OK.event: State constitution amended to prohibit state-level property taxation
Top rate cut from 7% to 5.5% over 2004-2009 period
HB 2764: brackets consolidated from 6 to 3; top rate to 4.5% in 2026 with trigger toward elimination

OR.event: Individual income tax enacted (one of earliest western states)
Measure 5 — caps property taxes; shifts school funding to state income tax
'Kicker' enshrined in constitution — tax revenue 2% above forecast must be refunded
Corporate Activity Tax takes effect — Oregon's first broad-based business tax beyond corporate income tax

PA.event: Individual income tax enacted at flat 2.8% (flat rate constitutionally required)
Corporate rate phasing from 9.99% to 4.99% by 2031 (currently 7.99%). Working Pennsylvanians Tax Credit (state EITC) enacted.

RI.event: Individual income tax enacted (originally piggyback on federal tax)
Major income tax reform — replaced old structure with three brackets (3.75%-5.99%)
PTET credit reduced to 90%. Decoupled from OBBBA tip/overtime exclusions.

SC.event: Top rate reduced from 7% to 6.5% with triggers for further reductions
Top rate cut to 6.0%. H.4216 proposes flat 3.99% for 2026 with path toward elimination.

SD.event: Sales tax enacted (no individual or corporate income tax ever enacted)
South Dakota v. Wayfair — US Supreme Court case allows states to collect sales tax from remote sellers
Sales tax rate reduced from 4.5% to 4.2% (temporary through June 2027)

UT.event: Income tax converted from graduated brackets to flat 5.0% rate
Flat rate reduced to 4.50% (from 4.55%) — fourth consecutive annual cut via HB 106

VT.event: Income tax enacted (replacing statewide property tax)
Sales tax enacted at 3% (last state in US to adopt a sales tax)
Act 71: expanded EITC, extended Child Tax Credit to age 6, created Veteran Tax Credit, new 0.44% employer childcare payroll tax

VA.event: Individual income tax enacted (one of the earliest states in the nation)
Retail Sales and Use Tax enacted at 2%
Rolling conformity replaced with fixed date. PTET made permanent. EITC raised to 20%. Selective deconformity from OBBBA.

WV.event: HB 2526 — largest income tax cut in state history (21.25% average reduction); top rate from 6.5% to 5.12%
SB 2033 further reduces rates; five brackets now 2.22%-4.82%. Social Security exemption phasing to 100% by 2026.

WY.event: Sales tax enacted as 'temporary emergency measure'
No income tax (constitutional barrier). Revenue dependent on mineral severance/sales taxes. Ranks #1 on Tax Foundation competitiveness index.
